acceptance_criteria: The validation readiness-evaluation workflow exits successfully.
The validation gate records passing evidence in status/readiness.json.
completion_signals: validate_build_pack_contract reaches pass state.
Validation evaluation evidence is recorded under eval/history and linked from status/readiness.json.
escalation_conditions: Validation reports schema or contract failures that the current task scope cannot resolve safely.
tasks.summary: Run the validation readiness-evaluation workflow and capture canonical validation evidence.

acceptance_criteria: The benchmark readiness-evaluation workflow exits successfully.
Inherited benchmark gates update from not_run to pass or waived.
completion_signals: Benchmark evaluation evidence records results in eval/latest/index.json.
Required benchmark gates advance to pass or waived in status/readiness.json.
escalation_conditions: Benchmark output fails to update readiness or eval state through the existing bounded surfaces.
tasks.summary: Run the benchmark readiness-evaluation workflow after validation passes.

acceptance_criteria: The build-pack records the exact artifact fields required for the first shallow surface map before command implementation starts.
The artifact contract keeps observed facts, tentative classification, and unknowns distinct.
completion_signals: The first-wave machine-readable artifact contract is described in a pack-local spec or contract note.
The current pack-local objective, backlog, and work-state all point at the shallow surface map as the first product slice.
escalation_conditions: The contract expands into full dependency mapping or deep subsystem interpretation before the first artifact shape is stable.
The artifact cannot distinguish observed evidence from tentative inference.
tasks.summary: Confirm the first-wave shallow surface map artifact contract before implementing discovery commands.

acceptance_criteria: The build-pack can gather a first read-only shallow surface map from the live Rocky 8 host and appliance runtime.
The first pass stays intentionally shallow and stops after one machine-readable map plus one operator-reviewable summary.
Any imported ASMS documentation context remains a supplementary hint layer rather than replacing runtime evidence.
completion_signals: One first-wave artifact exists for the machine-readable shallow surface map.
One first-wave operator-reviewable summary exists and derives from the map.
The first pass names one or two deeper seams worth investigating next.
The target-backed run can mirror the generated shallow-surface artifacts into the selected autonomy run root so PackFactory can return them through the bounded runtime-evidence export/import path.
escalation_conditions: The implementation starts drifting into deep Java or dependency analysis before the first shallow artifact is accepted.
The first pass cannot stay read-only or bounded to host-and-runtime observable surfaces.
tasks.summary: Implement the first shallow read-only surface map slice for the ADF successor build pack, with supplementary ASMS doc-pack hints allowed only as ranking and naming support.

acceptance_criteria: The build-pack can derive bounded Apache edge-to-local handoff hints from the target without drifting into full dependency mapping.
Exact-PID listener ownership is preferred over fuzzy Java-name matching when linking local listener ports to target components.
The returned target-backed summary surfaces at least a few concrete route-to-local ownership hints for support-relevant families.
completion_signals: The machine-readable surface map includes bounded `edge_route_hints` derived from Apache config hints plus local listener ownership.
The operator-reviewable summary surfaces route-aware handoff hints such as `/BusinessFlow`, `/afa/external`, `/afa/api/v1`, or `/keycloak/` when visible.
A target-backed PackFactory roundtrip imports the improved route-aware artifacts back into eval history.
escalation_conditions: The route-hint implementation starts parsing whole config trees deeply or claiming full request-path ownership without bounded evidence.
Listener ownership cannot be improved beyond generic Java-name fallback and the produced route-owner claims become misleading.
tasks.summary: Add a bounded route-aware seam to the successor by linking Apache proxy hints to exact local listener ownership on the target.

acceptance_criteria: The successor can attribute listeners held by wrapped child processes back to the owning service record when the process inventory shows a bounded parent-child chain.
Generic fallback matching no longer causes unrelated services like Elasticsearch to absorb broad Java listener sets.
A target-backed proof shows port 1989 linked to the AFF service family through descendant listener ownership.
completion_signals: The machine-readable map records `descendant_pid` listener ownership where a service wrapper launches the real Java listener process.
The target-backed imported proof shows `aff-boot` carrying port `1989` and reduces false listener spillover onto unrelated Java services.
The imported route-aware proof preserves AFF Apache route hints while narrowing the local listener owner.
escalation_conditions: Ownership attribution would require broad process ancestry crawling or mutation-heavy probes beyond the bounded read-only slice.
The target process tree changes too quickly for bounded descendant attribution to remain trustworthy.
tasks.summary: Improve target listener ownership so wrapped child Java processes can be attributed back to the owning service and generic Java-name spillover is reduced.

acceptance_criteria: The next bounded seam confirms whether the Apache `aff.conf` family plus local port `1989` is enough to treat `aff-boot` as the first FireFlow route owner.
The proof stays at route-owner and local-boundary level instead of widening into full FireFlow workflow dependency theory.
The resulting artifacts clearly separate confirmed local route ownership from still-tentative subsystem role inference.
completion_signals: A target-backed successor artifact set shows the AFF or FireFlow Apache family, the local `1989` listener, and the owning local service boundary in one bounded packet.
The next summary can explain the `1989` handoff in plain support language without reverting to generic Java or Elasticsearch guesses.
The successor names the next deeper FireFlow seam, if needed, from that bounded ownership proof rather than from broad architecture inference.
escalation_conditions: The next step would require full request replay, deep code-path tracing, or broad FireFlow subsystem theory before the bounded route-owner packet is stable.
Apache-side evidence and local-listener evidence diverge in a way that cannot be reconciled with the current read-only slice.
tasks.summary: Confirm the AFF or FireFlow route-owner boundary around Apache `aff.conf` and local port `1989` without widening into full dependency mapping.

acceptance_criteria: The next bounded seam compares the fronted `/FireFlow/api/session` path with the direct local `http://localhost:1989/aff/api/external/session` path without widening into full FireFlow theory.
The proof records whether the Apache-fronted AFF session path and the direct aff-boot session path agree on basic status and body shape.
If the parity check diverges, the successor stops at the Apache-to-aff-boot boundary and records that divergence plainly instead of widening into unrelated subsystem speculation.
completion_signals: A bounded packet or proof report captures the fronted AFF session path, the direct aff-boot session path, and whether their observable responses align.
The next summary can explain the AFF session seam in plain support language instead of only naming the local route owner.
The successor identifies whether the next stop after parity should be a UserSession-style bridge seam or whether the boundary still needs to stay at Apache-to-aff-boot.
escalation_conditions: The next step would require broad request replay, authentication bypass, or mutation-heavy probes before a bounded fronted-vs-direct AFF session check is stable.
The fronted AFF session path and the direct aff-boot session path disagree in a way that cannot be explained with a bounded read-only route packet.
tasks.summary: Validate the AFF session route parity between Apache `/FireFlow/api/session` and the direct aff-boot local session path on port `1989` without widening into deeper FireFlow internals yet.

acceptance_criteria: The next bounded seam stays behind the already-confirmed AFF session route and identifies whether FireFlow `UserSession` is the next support-useful stop.
The proof uses bounded retained evidence such as FireFlow log signatures or nearby session markers rather than widening into generic FireFlow architecture theory.
If the bounded UserSession bridge evidence is weak or stale, the successor records that clearly and stops there instead of manufacturing a broader subsystem map.
completion_signals: A bounded proof or packet names the UserSession-style bridge behind the AFF session route using concrete retained evidence.
The next summary can explain the post-parity stop in plain support language, such as `UserSession`, reused FA session id, or similar retained session markers.
The successor keeps the seam narrow and does not reopen Keycloak, ActiveMQ, or broad FireFlow internals without stronger evidence.
escalation_conditions: The next step would require full workflow replay, broad code-path tracing, or mutation-heavy probes before a bounded UserSession bridge packet is stable.
The available retained FireFlow evidence is too stale or too thin to support a named post-parity bridge without over-claiming.
tasks.summary: Inspect the FireFlow UserSession-style bridge behind the now-confirmed AFF session route without widening into general FireFlow subsystem theory.

acceptance_criteria: The next bounded seam stays behind the now-visible UserSession bridge and checks whether retained evidence really supports a reused FA-session chain.
The proof uses bounded retained markers such as `Using existing FASessionId`, `ff-session`, or closely adjacent UserSession request traces instead of widening into generic FireFlow workflow theory.
If the retained evidence is too stale or too ambiguous to support the reuse chain, the successor records that clearly and stops there.
completion_signals: A bounded proof or packet shows whether the visible UserSession bridge also carries a reused FA-session-style chain.
The next summary can explain the post-UserSession seam in plain support language, such as reused FA session id or retained session-reuse markers.
The successor keeps the seam narrow and does not reopen Keycloak, ActiveMQ, or broad FireFlow internals without stronger evidence.
escalation_conditions: The next step would require full workflow replay, broad code-path tracing, or mutation-heavy probes before a bounded reused-session packet is stable.
The retained UserSession evidence is too stale or too thin to support a reused FA-session claim without over-claiming.
tasks.summary: Trace the reused FA-session chain behind the now-visible FireFlow UserSession bridge without widening into broader FireFlow workflow theory.

acceptance_criteria: The next bounded seam stays just upstream of the now-confirmed reused FA-session chain and checks which BusinessFlow or AFA-side caller appears to feed or refresh that session context.
The proof uses tightly scoped retained markers or route-adjacent support evidence rather than widening into full login or cookie-bootstrap reconstruction.
If the upstream caller evidence is too thin, the successor records that clearly and stops there instead of manufacturing a cleaner session-origin story.
completion_signals: A bounded proof or packet shows whether the reused FireFlow FA-session chain can be tied to an upstream BusinessFlow or AFA-side session-origin clue.
The next summary can explain the post-reuse ambiguity in plain support language, such as upstream session-origin caller still thin or likely BusinessFlow to AFA refresh path.
The successor keeps the seam narrow and does not reopen generic suite-login, Keycloak, or broad FireFlow workflow theory without stronger evidence.
escalation_conditions: The next step would require full browser replay, broad cookie/bootstrap tracing, or mutation-heavy probes before a bounded upstream session-origin packet is stable.
The retained origin-side evidence is too stale or too thin to support a BusinessFlow or AFA caller claim without over-claiming.
tasks.summary: Trace the upstream BusinessFlow or AFA-side session-origin clue behind the now-confirmed reused FA-session chain without widening into full login or cookie-bootstrap theory.

acceptance_criteria: The next bounded seam decides whether the retained upstream session-origin window is better read as original bootstrap or later shared polling.

The proof stays narrow: use retained Apache and source-side clues around `getAFASessionInfo`, `CommandsDispatcher`, `storeFireflowCookie`, `bridge/refresh`, or the nearby BusinessFlow and AFF health family rather than widening into full browser replay.
If the retained evidence still cannot cleanly separate bootstrap from upkeep, the successor records that uncertainty plainly and stops there.
completion_signals: A bounded proof or packet explains whether the current upstream origin clue is closer to bootstrap or to later shared polling.
The next summary can explain the distinction in plain support language, such as shared polling window or stronger bootstrap clue still missing.
The successor keeps the seam narrow and does not reopen generic login, Keycloak, or deep FireFlow workflow theory without stronger evidence.
escalation_conditions: The next step would require full browser replay, broad cookie reconstruction, or mutation-heavy probes before a bounded bootstrap-versus-polling packet is stable.
The retained bootstrap-versus-upkeep evidence is too stale or too mixed to support a clean classification without over-claiming.
tasks.summary: Distinguish the upstream session-origin clue as original bootstrap versus later shared polling without widening into full cookie-bootstrap reconstruction.

acceptance_criteria: The next bounded seam follows the now-visible bootstrap anchor into the AFF cookie handoff without widening into full browser replay or generic login reconstruction.

The proof stays narrow: use retained `storeFireflowCookie` markers, the visible `/afa/external//bridge/storeFireflowCookie` call, session-token handoff clues, and the already-confirmed AFF route-owner boundary rather than mutation-heavy probes.
If the retained handoff evidence is still too thin to connect the bootstrap anchor to the AFF cookie surface cleanly, the successor records that uncertainty plainly and stops there.
completion_signals: A bounded proof or packet explains how the current bootstrap anchor reaches the AFF cookie handoff surface.
The next summary can explain the cookie-handoff seam in plain support language, such as `storeFireflowCookie` handoff, reused session token carry-forward, or still-thin cookie transfer evidence.
The successor keeps the seam narrow and does not reopen generic login, Keycloak, or broad suite architecture theory without stronger evidence.
escalation_conditions: The next step would require full browser replay, broad cookie reconstruction, or mutation-heavy probes before a bounded AFF cookie-handoff packet is stable.
The retained cookie-handoff evidence is too stale or too mixed to support a clean bootstrap-anchor carry-forward claim without over-claiming.
tasks.summary: Inspect the AFF cookie handoff behind the now-separated bootstrap anchor and polling tail without widening into full login reconstruction.

acceptance_criteria: The next bounded seam separates the visible Java-heavy service families into support-useful runtime clusters without widening into deep dependency mapping or JVM attach-style inspection.
The proof stays narrow: use already-visible service units, listener ownership, JVM flags, catalina bases, and repeated route ownership rather than mutation-heavy probes or full thread-level analysis.
If the current evidence cannot cleanly separate the cluster boundaries, the successor records that ambiguity plainly and stops there.
completion_signals: A bounded proof or packet explains whether `ms-bflow`, `ms-metro`, `algosec-ms`, `aff-boot`, and adjacent Java-heavy services belong to clearly separate runtime clusters or still share an unresolved boundary.
The next summary can explain the Java cluster distinction in plain support language, such as separate Tomcat bases, shared messaging adjacency, or unresolved shared JVM boundary.
The successor keeps the seam narrow and does not reopen generic login, Keycloak, or broad suite architecture theory without stronger evidence.
escalation_conditions: The next step would require JVM attach tooling, mutation-heavy probes, or broad dependency crawling before a bounded runtime-cluster packet is stable.
The current listener and process evidence stays too mixed to support cluster-level claims without over-claiming.
tasks.summary: Inspect the bounded Java runtime clusters behind the now-bounded session-origin chain without widening into deep dependency mapping.

acceptance_criteria: The successor records one bounded architecture review of the observed ASMS runtime using collected evidence rather than speculative full-suite diagrams.
The review separates edge, service-family, identity, data, messaging, and integration seams clearly enough for later diagnostic mapping work.
The review stays honest about uncertainty and does not claim full dependency order or total product understanding.
completion_signals: A pack-local architecture review artifact exists and names the most visible runtime eras, module families, and shared substrates.
The next summary can explain the current ASMS build quality and runtime shape in plain operator language without over-claiming architectural cleanliness.
The review explicitly distinguishes observed fact from inference and future questions.
escalation_conditions: The next step would require broad codebase archaeology, deep dependency crawling, or unsupported target mutation before a bounded architecture review is useful.
The current retained runtime evidence is still too thin to separate observed architecture seams from guesswork.
tasks.summary: Produce a bounded ASMS runtime architecture review from successor evidence so later mapping work starts from visible seams instead of folklore.

acceptance_criteria: The successor records a bounded ranking of likely ASMS failure seams or choke points grounded in observed runtime topology, failed services, route ownership, and shared substrates.
The ranking stays explicit about evidence strength and separates likely support-visible failure seams from deeper architectural risk seams.
The analysis avoids pretending the current lab proof is enough to certify production-grade reliability.
completion_signals: A pack-local failure-seam review artifact exists and ranks a small set of likely failure or choke-point categories.
The next summary can explain why those seams matter in support or runtime terms without flattening them into one generic risk list.
The ranking keeps build-quality commentary clearly subordinate to the actual observed evidence.
escalation_conditions: The next step would require broad chaos testing, write-heavy probes, or unsupported production-style load experiments before a bounded failure-seam review is possible.
The current evidence is too sparse to rank likely failure seams honestly.
tasks.summary: Rank the most likely ASMS failure seams and choke points from observed runtime evidence as a bounded future analysis surface.

acceptance_criteria: The successor records a bounded list of likely support-engineer confusion points or pain seams grounded in the observed gap between product-facing paths and runtime-facing owners.
The resulting view helps explain why certain incidents may feel ambiguous from support without collapsing into full playbook generation.
The analysis stays operator-useful and does not require broad workflow simulation or deep incident reconstruction.
completion_signals: A pack-local support-pain-point review artifact exists and names a small set of likely support-visible ambiguity seams.
The next summary can explain those pain points in plain support language without forcing a complete support process redesign.
The artifact keeps support confusion points separate from pure architecture or reliability commentary.
escalation_conditions: The next step would require broad ticket mining, incident-history ingestion, or speculative customer-behavior claims before a bounded support-pain review is useful.
The current runtime evidence cannot distinguish support confusion from generic architecture complexity honestly.
tasks.summary: Capture likely ASMS support-engineer pain points from successor evidence as a bounded future analysis surface distinct from playbook generation.

acceptance_criteria: The successor can locate service and process config surfaces and log surfaces more reliably than the current command-line-only first pass.
The expanded discovery still stays bounded and read-only, preferring path discovery and shallow metadata over deep parsing or open-ended log mining.
Observed config and log locations stay clearly labeled as observed, candidate, or unresolved.
completion_signals: The machine-readable surface map records stronger config-path and log-path candidates for central services where available.
The next summary can explain where a human would inspect next for a given module or process family without pretending the logs or configs were fully interpreted.
The successor still keeps config parsing and log interpretation bounded behind explicit later slices.
escalation_conditions: The next step would require broad log scraping, unsupported privilege escalation, or deep config-tree parsing before bounded path discovery is stable.
The target surfaces do not expose path clues cleanly enough to improve discovery without over-claiming.
tasks.summary: Expand bounded read-only discovery so the successor can locate service and process configs and logs more reliably for later mapping work.

acceptance_criteria: The successor can derive bounded configuration-pattern and tunable-behavior hints for observed ASMS modules or services without confusing generic documentation with target-local fact.
The resulting hints separate observed settings, repeated runtime patterns, and still-unproven behavior suggestions clearly.
The analysis stays useful for later support or diagnostic work without turning into unsupported broad recommendations.
completion_signals: A pack-local inference artifact exists for module configuration patterns, tunable services, and likely behavior controls with confidence labels.
The next summary can explain which knobs look real, which look repeated, and which still need confirmation.
The successor keeps observed runtime evidence authoritative over inferred configuration guidance.
escalation_conditions: The next step would require unsupported broad configuration mutation, speculative vendor claims, or deep reverse engineering before bounded inference is useful.
The current config and log evidence is too thin to separate observed tunables from guesswork honestly.
tasks.summary: Infer bounded configuration patterns, tunable services, and behavior hints from observed ASMS evidence without elevating folklore above runtime fact.

acceptance_criteria: The successor has a bounded next-step plan for mapping distributed ASMS nodes and for layering version-matched external knowledge onto observed components or integrations.
The external knowledge model keeps online open-source component data and external-system vendor data subordinate to observed ASMS runtime evidence.
The resulting plan explicitly includes integration-side reasoning for external systems such as Fortinet or Panorama when those surfaces are observed.
completion_signals: A pack-local planning artifact exists for distributed-node mapping, open-source component knowledge layering, and external-system integration knowledge layering.
The next summary can explain how future multi-node and external-integration reasoning should stay evidence-first, version-aware, and fail-closed.
The plan names the difference between observed node reality, version-matched component guidance, and external-system behavior hints.
escalation_conditions: The next step would require uncontrolled web research, unsupported remote expansion, or unbounded cross-product speculation before a bounded plan is stable.
The current single-node evidence base is too thin to say anything honest about distributed topology or external integration behavior layering.
tasks.summary: Plan the next bounded extension from single-node ASMS mapping into distributed-node mapping plus version-matched open-source and external-system knowledge layering.

acceptance_criteria: The successor records one reusable second-node request shape that reuses the current remote-owned runtime and bounded import roundtrip instead of widening the current node-local map.
The build pack distinguishes node-local artifact fields from the later thin cross-node envelope fields explicitly.
The new request shape keeps PackFactory root canonical while allowing `adf-dev` to keep operational ownership of the runtime loop.
completion_signals: A pack-local artifact exists for the second-node request shape and its relationship to the later thin cross-node envelope.
The next summary can explain what belongs in a node-local map versus the cross-node envelope without mixing the two.
The current target-backed knowledge-layer proof is referenced as the reason the cross-node envelope remains inactive until a second node bundle exists.
escalation_conditions: The next step would require inventing a second lab target, widening the current node-local map, or claiming cross-node topology before a real second imported proof exists.
The current remote-owned runtime flow cannot express a second-node request without breaking the bounded export and import contract.
tasks.summary: Define the reusable second-node request shape and the thin cross-node envelope boundary without widening the current node-local successor map.

acceptance_criteria: The successor records one bounded follow-on readout of provider-specific integration evidence from the current node without claiming integration health or a full vendor behavior model.
The build pack separates observed AWS or Azure-side local markers from the larger dormant vendor inventory clearly.
The next summary can explain what additional config, route, or log signals would be needed to strengthen or reject the currently activated provider hints.
completion_signals: A pack-local artifact exists for provider-specific integration evidence on the current node.
The next summary can explain why AWS and Azure are activated locally while other vendor families remain dormant.
The current target-backed knowledge-layer proof remains the canonical baseline for what is observed versus still only hinted.
escalation_conditions: The next step would require vendor-side web folklore, broad reverse engineering, or claims about integration health that the node-local proof still does not support.
The current node lacks any stronger provider-side config, route, or log surfaces beyond the already-observed activation hints.
tasks.summary: Capture one bounded provider-specific integration-evidence readout from the current node so activated vendor hints stay tied to observed local surfaces.

acceptance_criteria: The successor captures one real second node-local proof bundle through the existing remote-owned runtime and bounded import roundtrip instead of inventing a suite graph.
The second node readout stays node-local first and only activates a thin cross-node envelope for fields that two imported proofs can honestly support.
The next summary can explain what changed across the two nodes without claiming full distributed topology or total suite dependency order.
If the next available node is standalone and lacks target-local Codex, the proof still runs cleanly from `adf-dev` through a node-specific read-only target-connection profile.
completion_signals: A real second-node run request and wrapper request are instantiated from the reusable templates.
One second imported node-local proof bundle exists under eval/history for this successor line.
The next summary can explain the bounded cross-node delta and whether the thin cross-node envelope is now activatable.
The returned proof states plainly whether the captured node should be treated as standalone calibration evidence or as part of a later distributed-lab topology picture.
escalation_conditions: The next step would require inventing a second lab target, claiming merged topology before a real second imported proof exists, or widening the successor into a full suite graph.
The current remote-owned runtime flow cannot express the second-node capture without breaking the bounded export and import contract.
tasks.summary: Capture one second node-local proof bundle through the existing remote-owned runtime flow so the successor can activate a thin cross-node envelope without inventing suite topology.

acceptance_criteria: The successor captures additional standalone-node proofs through the same `adf-dev`-owned bounded roundtrip without requiring target-local Codex on those nodes.
The resulting review keeps those nodes clearly labeled as independent calibration nodes rather than distributed-role proof.
The next summary can explain which runtime families repeat across independent nodes and which appear node-specific.
completion_signals: At least one additional imported standalone-node proof exists beyond the first second-node capture.
The current node-local artifact shape remains reusable across those nodes without ad hoc workflow drift.
The successor can now compare repeated families and repeated packaging patterns across a small independent-node set.
escalation_conditions: The next step would require target-local Codex installation, unsupported remote mutation, or custom per-node workflows before a reusable standalone-node calibration pass is stable.
The available standalone nodes differ so sharply that the successor cannot compare them honestly with the current node-local artifact shape.
tasks.summary: Capture a small standalone-node calibration set through `adf-dev` so the successor can test its node-local model across independent nodes.

acceptance_criteria: The successor can compare the imported standalone-node proofs and derive a bounded node archetype readout without claiming distributed topology.
The comparison distinguishes repeated families, optional families, and unusual node-local variants clearly.
The resulting readout remains support-useful and deployment-useful without pretending to know east-west traffic or cluster truth.
completion_signals: A bounded node-archetype comparison artifact exists across the standalone-node calibration set.
The next summary can explain what looks common versus node-specific across independent installs.
The successor names what evidence is still missing before distributed-topology claims become honest.
escalation_conditions: The next step would require inventing inter-node links or distributed roles that the standalone-node proofs do not support.
The current standalone-node proof set is too small or too inconsistent to support even a bounded archetype comparison honestly.
tasks.summary: Derive a bounded node archetype comparison from the standalone-node calibration set before activating distributed-topology work.

acceptance_criteria: The successor captures one or more node-local proofs from a deliberately distributed lab so topology and role separation are grounded in real multi-node evidence rather than independent standalone comparisons.
The review keeps role labels, cross-node endpoint hints, and unresolved edges clearly separated.
The execution model still uses `adf-dev` as the runtime owner and PackFactory as the canonical import authority.
completion_signals: At least one distributed-lab node-local proof exists beyond the standalone calibration set.
The next summary can explain what changed qualitatively once the successor moved from independent nodes into role-separated distributed evidence.
The thin cross-node envelope now has enough real material to activate honestly.
escalation_conditions: The next step would require guessing distributed roles, inventing a lab shape, or claiming topology before role-separated proof actually exists.
The distributed-lab setup cannot be observed through the existing bounded node-local request and import model.
tasks.summary: Capture role-separated distributed-lab node proofs so the successor can move from independent-node comparison into honest topology work.

acceptance_criteria: The successor can compare at least two imported node-local proof bundles and record a bounded cross-node topology surface without pretending to know the whole suite.
The topology output distinguishes observed node-local facts, cross-node similarities, and inferred role or adjacency hints clearly.
The resulting surface stays thin enough to remain evidence-first and fail-closed.
completion_signals: A bounded topology artifact exists that explains what appears shared, node-specific, or cross-node adjacent across imported proofs.
The next summary can explain the first honest multi-node picture without widening into a full dependency graph or total suite theory.
The successor keeps unseen nodes, links, and roles explicitly out of scope.
escalation_conditions: The next step would require inventing additional nodes, hidden routes, or unseen links before a bounded topology surface is stable.
The imported node-local proofs are too thin or too mismatched to support even a fail-closed topology readout honestly.
tasks.summary: Activate a bounded multi-node topology map once at least two imported node-local proofs exist.

acceptance_criteria: The successor can derive a fuller dependency graph from repeated imported proofs, route evidence, listener ownership, config surfaces, and retained runtime hints.
The graph output distinguishes observed edges from inferred edges clearly.
The result improves support-useful dependency reasoning without pretending full graph completeness too early.
completion_signals: A dependency-focused artifact exists for the strongest observed and inferred runtime edges.
The next summary can explain which dependencies are directly evidenced, which are strongly inferred, and which remain unknown.
The successor avoids flattening adjacency, route hints, and real dependencies into one undifferentiated graph.
escalation_conditions: The next step would require unsupported deep reverse engineering or unsupported suite-wide claims before bounded dependency edges are stable.
The current imported proof base cannot separate true dependency edges from loose co-location or repeated adjacency honestly.
tasks.summary: Derive a fuller dependency graph from imported runtime proof once topology mapping is active.

acceptance_criteria: The successor can say more than whether an integration appears present by recording bounded health states such as configured, reachable, healthy, degraded, or uncertain.
Health claims stay tied to runtime, config, route, or log evidence rather than folklore or generic vendor expectations.
The output keeps provider-specific and product-specific integrations explicit instead of collapsing them into one health bucket.
completion_signals: A health-validated integration artifact exists for the strongest currently observed integration families.
The next summary can explain which integrations appear merely present versus measurably healthy or degraded.
Unknown or weakly-supported integrations remain explicitly unresolved.
escalation_conditions: The next step would require speculative health claims, uncontrolled mutation, or vendor-side assumptions before bounded health evidence is stable.
The current proof surfaces cannot distinguish configuration presence from runtime health honestly.
tasks.summary: Capture a health-validated integration model once the successor can lean on stronger topology and dependency evidence.

acceptance_criteria: The successor can explain larger end-to-end product behavior across major request, session, service, and integration flows without pretending the entire suite is fully known.
The resulting behavior model stays grounded in imported proofs and clearly labeled inference.
The output is support-useful and diagnostic-useful rather than becoming an unsupported product-theory document.
completion_signals: A behavior-model artifact exists that ties together the strongest proven seams into a higher-level product behavior surface.
The next summary can explain how important flows appear to behave, where the proof is strong, and where uncertainty still remains.
The successor still separates observed packets, inferred behavior, and unknowns cleanly.
escalation_conditions: The next step would require a polished but unsupported suite story before enough runtime proof exists.
The available evidence cannot yet support end-to-end behavior claims without blurring observed fact and inference.
tasks.summary: Derive a more complete product behavior model once the successor has stronger topology, dependency, and integration-health evidence.

acceptance_criteria: The successor records one bounded follow-on provider-health readout for AWS and Azure using local service, listener, and recent journal evidence on the distributed CM and remote-agent nodes.
The output can distinguish configured, reachable, and degraded more honestly for the local provider-driver services without turning local runtime trouble into unsupported provider-side claims.
Provider API success, credential correctness, sync freshness, and cross-node directionality remain explicit non-claims unless directly proven.
completion_signals: A deeper provider-health artifact exists and cites the existing health model plus the fresh distributed CM and remote-agent imports as its proof base.
The next summary can explain which provider-driver families are merely present versus locally degraded.
Unsafe claims about cloud-side success or full distributed provider health remain explicit non-claims.
escalation_conditions: The next step would require vendor-side assumptions, uncontrolled probing, or claims about external API success that the current local proof still does not support.
The available local signals cannot honestly distinguish configured placement from local reachability or degradation.
tasks.summary: Capture one deeper bounded provider-health proof for the visible AWS and Azure driver families without promoting local runtime trouble into unsupported cloud-side health claims.

acceptance_criteria: The successor can say more about how provider-facing work is divided across the CM and remote-agent nodes without inventing hidden cross-node links.
Any directionality claim stays clearly separated into observed local facts, repeated cross-node patterns, and bounded inference.
The result stays narrower than a full distributed dependency or provider-success model.
completion_signals: A bounded cross-node directionality artifact exists for the strongest repeated provider-facing families.
The next summary can explain what appears CM-led, RA-led, shared, or still unresolved in the provider-facing split.
The successor keeps cloud-side success and unseen east-west edges explicitly unresolved where proof is still thin.
escalation_conditions: The next step would require claiming hidden east-west flows or internal orchestration direction without sufficient imported node-local proof.
The available proof remains too symmetric to support an honest directionality readout.
tasks.summary: Strengthen the cross-node directionality proof for the repeated provider-facing families across the CM and remote-agent nodes.
